title: Azure AI Cloud Developer Associate (AI-200)
subtitle: Build, Integrate, and Monitor AI Solutions on Azure
description: Learn to build scalable AI solutions on Azure, covering infrastructure design, containerised deployments, API management, and production monitoring for AI workloads
overview: This intermediate course prepares you for the AI-200 certification exam by covering the full spectrum of building and operating AI solutions on Azure cloud infrastructure. You will learn to design scalable AI architectures using AKS and serverless compute, manage AI service APIs with Azure API Management, implement vector-enabled databases for semantic search, build event-driven AI pipelines, and monitor production AI workloads for performance and cost. The course bridges the gap between AI model development and production cloud engineering.

learningObjectives.title: AI Infrastructure & Architecture
learningObjectives.description: Design scalable AI architectures using AKS, serverless functions, and containerised compute for inference and training workloads

learningObjectives.title: Building & Deploying AI Solutions
learningObjectives.description: Build event-driven AI pipelines, integrate vector-enabled databases, manage AI service APIs, and deploy AI solutions with secure secret management

learningObjectives.title: Monitoring & Cost Optimisation
learningObjectives.description: Implement distributed observability for AI workloads and optimise inference costs with scaling strategies and resource right-sizing

modules.title: AI Infrastructure & Architecture
modules.description: Design and provision Azure infrastructure for AI workloads, from compute selection to networking and storage
topics: Scalable AI architecture patterns on Azure
AKS for AI workloads: GPU node pools, scheduling, and autoscaling
Containerised compute for model inference and batch processing
Serverless functions for lightweight AI integrations
Virtual networking for AI service isolation and security
Storage strategies for training data, embeddings, and model artefacts
Infrastructure as Code with Bicep for AI environments
Secret management with Azure Key Vault for API keys and credentials

modules.title: Building & Deploying AI Solutions
modules.description: Build production AI solutions with event-driven pipelines, vector databases, and API management
topics: API Management for AI services: throttling, caching, and versioning
Vector-enabled databases: Azure Cosmos DB and Azure AI Search
Event-driven AI pipelines with Event Grid and Service Bus
Building AI microservices with container apps and AKS
Integrating Azure OpenAI Service into application backends
CI/CD pipelines for AI solution deployments
Blue-green and canary deployment strategies for AI endpoints
Authentication and authorisation patterns for AI APIs

modules.title: Monitoring & Cost Optimisation
modules.description: Implement observability for AI workloads and optimise costs across inference, storage, and compute
topics: Distributed observability with Azure Monitor and Application Insights
Custom metrics and dashboards for AI inference performance
Log analytics for AI request tracing and error diagnosis
Alert rules for latency, error rates, and token consumption
Cost optimisation strategies for inference workloads
Right-sizing compute for AI: spot instances and reserved capacity
Token usage analysis and model selection for cost efficiency
Budgets, cost alerts, and chargeback for AI projects

handsOnLabs.title: AKS Deployment for AI Inference
handsOnLabs.description: Deploy a containerised AI model on AKS with GPU node pools, autoscaling, and health monitoring

handsOnLabs.title: Vector Database Integration
handsOnLabs.description: Set up a vector-enabled database with Azure Cosmos DB, ingest embeddings, and query with semantic search

handsOnLabs.title: API Management for AI Services
handsOnLabs.description: Configure Azure API Management to front AI endpoints with rate limiting, caching, and usage analytics

handsOnLabs.title: AI Workload Monitoring Dashboard
handsOnLabs.description: Build a custom Azure Monitor dashboard tracking inference latency, token consumption, and cost trends

prerequisites: Programming experience with Python, C#, or JavaScript
Understanding of cloud architecture concepts and patterns
Basic familiarity with containers and Kubernetes
targetAudience: Azure developers adding AI capabilities to existing solutions
DevOps engineers supporting AI workloads in production

instructor.name: Yair Knijn
instructor.title: Principal Cloud Architect & Engineer
instructor.bio: Microsoft Certified Trainer with extensive experience designing and operating AI solutions on Azure. Specialises in bridging the gap between AI development and production cloud infrastructure, with a focus on performance, cost, and reliability.
certifications: Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert

certification.name: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Cloud Developer Associate

personalStory.story: Most AI courses stop at the model. But I have seen too many proof-of-concept demos that never survived contact with production traffic, cost constraints, or a Monday morning spike. This course exists because the hard part of AI is not calling an API -- it is running that API reliably at scale without burning through your Azure budget. I teach what I have learned from running AI workloads in production: the infrastructure decisions, the monitoring that actually matters, and the cost traps you only discover after the first invoice.
